action.content:
Let us do the following task. The goal is to fill an empty grid that looks like this:

‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ

A filled grid below is 5 by 5 and can look like this:

‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ E ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
X X X X X

I want you to describe this grid to me, step by step. You don't need to describe the empty squares, which are denoted with "‚ĖĘ". Only describe the location of letters in the grid. Then you wait for me to say "What is your next command?", and then you continue with the next step. Take the size of the grid into consideration while giving commands. You can give only one command at each turn but it can refer to single or multiple cells, rows, columns or even the whole grid at once. When you have described everything, you say "Command: DONE". Only give the command and omit everything else.

For the filled grid above, here are the example steps.

Command: Put an E in second row third column

What is your next command?
Command: Fill the last row with X

What is your next command?
Command: DONE

Ok. Please do this for the following example, which is a 5 by 5 grid.
T ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
T T ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
T T T ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
T T T T ‚ĖĘ
T T T T T
What is your next command?
action.label: context

action.content: Command: Put a T in the first column of the first four rows
action.label: response

action.content: Command: Put a T in the first column of the first four rows

Let us draw something together. There is an empty grid with a size 5 by 5, like so:

‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ

I will give you commands like "put an X in the top left", and you return the grid by applying the given command, like so:

Command: put an X in the top left

X ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ

Now create an empty grid with a size 5 by 5 and execute the following commands at each step. Once you execute the command, return only the grid and exclude all other text from the output.

Command: Put a T in the first column of the first four rows
